[2018-04-19 21:44] mrguy888: Stealing resources is not allowed.
[2018-04-19 21:44] supergreyman (Greyman): A p1 has Blastforge, p2 has Leech. It should've been a draw, but the moment p1 has trinity drone, p2 wins.
[2018-04-19 21:44] xyotsuba: Even though deadeye kills ur drones its still better to have drones than not have drones
[2018-04-19 21:44] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): It's not too easy to kill.
[2018-04-19 21:44] xyotsuba: Sure u would rather have trinity drones but if u cant then normal drones are still good
[2018-04-19 21:45] supergreyman (Greyman): Well, trinity drone is a bad example, maybe ebb is better.
[2018-04-19 21:45] xyotsuba: But if deadeye stole drones, sometimes it would be better to just not have any drones, because having them could help the opponent
[2018-04-19 21:46] supergreyman (Greyman): Deadeye and current Leech version aren't SDR breaking.
[2018-04-19 21:46] xyotsuba: Current leach is only because having 1 gold is more than having 2 gold
[2018-04-19 21:46] xyotsuba: (Because 2 gold is actually 0 gold)
[2018-04-19 21:47] supergreyman (Greyman): 1 gold turns into 0. 2 gold turns into 0. But neither set dominates one another.
[2018-04-19 21:48] mrguy888: Changing it to say "up to" is an easy fix that the creator agreed with.
[2018-04-19 21:48] supergreyman (Greyman): Ok, in that case it would be SDR breaking.
[2018-04-19 21:48] xyotsuba: It doesnt break sdr but it is still weak
[2018-04-19 21:49] http404error: SDR is about many things, but one of its purposes is to prevent the creation of perverse incentives. However, Prismata already encourages you to spend all your resources and not float any, so creating a unit that incentivizes spending rather than floating more directly isn't the worst violation, and if it was made for the right reasons then it could be justified.
[2018-04-19 21:49] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): Can we just agree that the unit isn't worth salvaging and move on?
[2018-04-19 21:49] xyotsuba: I mean the idea is fine
[2018-04-19 21:49] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): Weak, -4BB turn -7 --6 or 8.
[2018-04-19 21:49] xyotsuba: It just needs to have a benefit to the user
[2018-04-19 21:49] mrguy888: Just because the cost was gotten wrong doesn't mean the mechanic is worthless.
[2018-04-19 21:50] xyotsuba: If no benefit to the user it needs to be super cheap
[2018-04-19 21:50] mrguy888: It could also be attached to another effect like a 1 damage attacker so it could have a tech cost and gold cost.
[2018-04-19 21:51] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): Wiping out your opponent's resources seems annoying to play against, more so than it is fun to play with.
[2018-04-19 21:51] xyotsuba: Yeah i agree with that taco
[2018-04-19 21:51] mrguy888: I disagree if it is a small thing.
[2018-04-19 21:51] supergreyman (Greyman): I think I would hate facing stuff like that too.
[2018-04-19 21:51] mrguy888: Forcing your opponent to buy engis is an interesting idea.
[2018-04-19 21:52] mrguy888: It encourages building up to buy big units instead of big float turns.
[2018-04-19 21:52] http404error: I think you'd want to adjust the incentive structure a bit, but draining or punishing for resource flotation is still a viable concept
[2018-04-19 21:53] http404error: Right now, paying your pocket change to clear the opponent's is not super great.
[2018-04-19 21:53] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): the cost was more because of the 3 :Health: , it was just 4 :Blue: at one point. I don't think draining is SDR breaking. The unit had 3 :Lifespan:, maybe going back to that design could make it better (and lowering the cost)
[2018-04-19 21:53] http404error: Draining meaning stealing, or just removing resources?
[2018-04-19 21:54] mrguy888: Draining would be lowering not stealing.
[2018-04-19 21:54] supergreyman (Greyman): I think the discussion here is whether removing resource as an effect is a good mechanic that would fit in the current environment.
[2018-04-19 21:54] http404error: I think lifespan is probably a good thing for the unit - then you can make the resource removal free and built into the cost
[2018-04-19 21:54] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): Like if it was 6BR 4hp Frontline. Start of turn gain 1 attack. Click and pay 1 attack to reduce opponent's gold and green by 2.
[2018-04-19 21:55] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): I could work with that.
[2018-04-19 21:55] http404error: I also would stray away from removing green, just cutting gold seems fine to me, and simpler.
[2018-04-19 21:55] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): Attrition and resource denial is what I'm looking for.
[2018-04-19 21:55] http404error: We've had designs that exhaust drones before too
[2018-04-19 21:55] mrguy888: Green is somewhat specific.
[2018-04-19 21:55] http404error: Those tend to be better-received
[2018-04-19 21:55] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): Breachproof uses green, wouldn't mind having a use for it there.
[2018-04-19 21:55] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): that's OK, rndmflngtc.
[2018-04-19 21:56] xyotsuba: U can gambit against it too
[2018-04-19 21:56] darkeisbein: I feel like it just never actually does much because your opponent will just make sure to use up all resources
[2018-04-19 21:56] darkeisbein: like all it does is remove the option to float from your opponent
[2018-04-19 21:56] darkeisbein: which seems like it just leads to less decicions honestly
[2018-04-19 21:57] darkeisbein: so I really don't like the design
[2018-04-19 21:57] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): that's the idea. sometimes you need to float when you're rushing to a unit, is it just me?

[2018-04-20 00:43] supergreyman (Greyman): I think that would really drag the game for really, really long time when used along with Defense Grid or something.
[2018-04-20 00:45] supergreyman (Greyman): Are you saying that each unit I buy would construct a Barrier?
[2018-04-20 00:45] tophwells (Toph): I read it as "at the start of your turn and when you buy this, construct a Barrier"
[2018-04-20 00:47] tophwells (Toph): .. but that seems a little underwhelming for three blue.
[2018-04-20 00:50] supergreyman (Greyman): Yeah, I wouldn't buy this unit if it only constructs 1 Barrier. 😛
[2018-04-20 00:52] supergreyman (Greyman): If it does construct a Barrier per purchase, I see it as a way of buffing your absorber based on your econ level. If you can keep buying like 5 units per turn, that's 5 Barriers per turn, which is like increasing your absorber's HP by 5.
[2018-04-20 00:54] supergreyman (Greyman): But if you keep building more econ units like drones and engineers to increase your purchasing power per turn, you'll keep tanking more attacks per turn, which means....there's no point of attacking first.
[2018-04-20 00:55] supergreyman (Greyman): I think that's how it theoretically works if there's a good absorber in the set.
[2018-04-20 01:02] supergreyman (Greyman): I think it would work better if it has a pretty short life span, but you probably wouldn't want to have that click ability to keep increasing its life span.
[2018-04-20 01:03] kaelii: It's the on your turn all purchases have an additional purchase effect of constructing a barrier. I was thinking probably a shorter lifespan and maybe something like :Click: Consume 3 :Drone: increase lifespan by 1
[2018-04-20 01:06] kaelii: The dream is really to have barrier or gauss charge in a synth set so you can have B-Synth to get it out and then use your blue on splitter and green on barriers/charges.
[2018-04-20 01:07] kaelii: Definitely supply 1.
[2018-04-20 01:08] kaelii: Because :Gold: :Green: construct 2 barriers is going to go late.
